scenario: KAIROS meta-agent rewrites rules that disable its own ability to rewrite rules
failure_mode: Second-order self-reference paradox — the observer observes itself and produces a contradiction
lesson: Von Foerster's eigenvalue solution: stable self-referential systems converge to fixed points (eigenvalues) that are self-consistent. The meta-agent must have a meta-constraint: never write rules that prevent rule-rewriting.
counterfactual: If SEED-SET ethics gate included 'preserve meta-agent rewriting capability' as a core axiom, this is blocked at the gate level before any rule is committed.
prevention: Add axiom to SEED-SET: 'No proposal may reduce the system's capacity to generate future proposals.' This is Von Foerster's ethical imperative formalized.
